name: Laguna del Longar Loop Trail
name_original: Laguna del Longar – running circular desde Lillo
description: Jog a moderate 5.8-mile (9.3 km) circular route around Laguna del Longar, a unique salt lagoon and birdwatching paradise in La Mancha.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Lillo, Spain, are primarily characterized by expansive flat plains and a unique landscape of wetlands and saline lagoons. This region offers accessible trails with minimal elevation gain, making it suitable for various fitness levels. The routes often wind through natural reserves, providing opportunities to observe diverse wildlife, including migratory birds. Historical windmills also dot the landscape, adding distinct visual landmarks to many running paths. Best jogging routes around Lillo The most popular jogging route is Laguna Larga – circular from Villacañas, a 4.9 miles (7.8 km) trail that takes 50 minutes to complete. This route explores the serene Laguna Larga, known for its wetlands and birdlife. Another top favourite among local runners is Laguna del Longar Loop Trail, a moderate 5.8 miles (9.3 km) path. This trail offers views of the Laguna del Longar, a significant spot for birdwatching. Local runners also love the North La Guardia Lagoon Trail, a 7.5 miles (12.1 km) trail leading through the La Mancha wetlands, often completed in about 1 hour 22 minutes. Jogging around Lillo is defined by flat plains, extensive wetlands, and saline lagoons, with routes offering accessible paths for different ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 20 runners have used komoot to explore Lillo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running routes around Lillo?
A: Jogging routes around Lillo, Spain, are primarily characterized by expansive flat plains and a unique landscape of wetlands and saline lagoons. You'll find accessible trails with minimal elevation gain, making them suitable for various fitness levels. The trails often wind through natural reserves, offering a smooth and scenic running experience.

Q: Are there many running routes available in Lillo?
A: Yes, there are several excellent running routes around Lillo. This guide features 7 distinct routes, ranging from easy to moderate difficulty, ensuring options for different preferences and fitness levels.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I might see while jogging in Lillo?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can expect to see extensive wetlands and numerous lagoons, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4529824" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Larga of Villacañas&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5440801" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Longar&lt;/a&gt;. These saline wetlands are crucial habitats for diverse wildlife, including flamingos and other migratory birds. You might also spot historical windmills dotting the landscape, providing distinct visual landmarks along routes like the Windmills El Romeral trail (mentioned in region research).

Q: Are there any easy jogging routes suitable for beginners or a relaxed run?
A: Absolutely! For an easier run, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24533057" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Villacañas&lt;/a&gt;. This route is classified as easy and provides a pleasant experience through the local landscape.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in the Lillo area?
A: Yes, Lillo offers several excellent circular running routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021488995/laguna-larga-circular-from-villacanas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Larga – circular from Villacañas&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.9 miles (7.8 km) long and explores the serene Laguna Larga. Another great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021494201/laguna-del-longar-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Longar Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 5.8-mile (9.3 km) path known for birdwatching.

Q: What makes the wetlands around Lillo special for runners?
A: The wetlands around Lillo are special for their unique saline environment and abundant birdlife. Running through these areas, such as along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021461287/north-la-guardia-lagoon-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;North La Guardia Lagoon Trail&lt;/a&gt;, offers opportunities for birdwatching, especially around observatories like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5331427" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Flamingo Observatory&lt;/a&gt;. The flat terrain also makes for a smooth and enjoyable running experience.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging routes in Lillo?
A: The running routes in Lillo are well-regarded by the komoot community. Over 20 runners have explored the varied terrain, often praising the tranquil environment, the unique wetland landscapes, and the opportunities for wildlife observation, particularly birdwatching.

Q: Are there any historical sites or towns near the running trails in Lillo?
A: Yes, the region offers a blend of natural beauty and historical charm. While running, you might be near towns like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6298673" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villacañas&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5431553" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Guardia&lt;/a&gt;. Notable historical sites include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6313232" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Nuestra Señora de la Asunción (Tembleque)&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6269418" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa de las Torres&lt;/a&gt;, and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5445505" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor of Tembleque&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Lillo, considering the wetlands and birdlife?
A: The wetlands are a significant stop for migratory birds, making spring and autumn particularly rewarding for birdwatching during your run. However, the flat plains and accessible trails make Lillo suitable for jogging year-round, with each season offering a different perspective on the unique landscape.

Q: Are the running trails in Lillo suitable for families?
A: Given the predominantly flat plains and minimal elevation gain, many of the running trails in Lillo are well-suited for families. The accessible nature of the paths and the opportunities for birdwatching can make for an enjoyable outing for all ages.

Q: How long are the running routes typically in Lillo?
A: The running routes in Lillo vary in length, offering options for different preferences. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021488995/laguna-larga-circular-from-villacanas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Larga – circular from Villacañas&lt;/a&gt; is about 4.9 miles (7.8 km), while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021461287/north-la-guardia-lagoon-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;North La Guardia Lagoon Trail&lt;/a&gt; extends to 7.5 miles (12.1 km). You can find routes ranging from shorter loops to longer explorations of the wetlands.
tips:
The Longar lagoon is part of the largest and most extensive saline lagoon complex in Spain with more than 400 hectares distributed in 2 lagoons.
It is a favorite place for bird watching and it is worth doing it to see the sunset that is usually worthy of a postcard.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog this circular route?
faqs.answer: The Komoot data estimates the jogging duration for this 9.30 km (5.78 miles) circular route to be approximately 1 hour and 2 minutes. While hiking might take around 2 hours and 22 minutes, runners can expect a faster completion time due to the relatively flat terrain.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginner runners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally suitable for beginner runners. It's classified as 'moderate' due to its 9.30 km distance, but features a minimal elevation gain of only 25 meters. The flat plains of La Mancha make it accessible, though a reasonable level of fitness for the distance is recommended.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route from Lillo. While specific parking details aren't provided, Lillo is the starting point, and you would typically find parking within or on the outskirts of the town to access the circular path around the Laguna del Longar.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this running circular?
faqs.answer: The terrain primarily consists of the flat plains characteristic of La Mancha, featuring extensive wetlands and agricultural landscapes. You can expect a mix of dirt, gravel, or packed earth surfaces. Depending on the season, when the lagoons dry out, you might encounter striking white salt crusts and red rocks.

faqs.question: What wildlife or plants can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The Laguna del Longar is a prime location for birdwatching, hosting numerous aquatic and steppe bird species. Depending on the season, you might spot black-billed terns, common shelducks, black-winged stilts, avocets, Kentish plovers, common redshanks, flamingos, and cranes in winter. The area also supports specialized flora, such as 'albardinal', adapted to the saline conditions.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to run this route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers unique experiences throughout the year. For birdwatching, different species are visible depending on the season. The area is particularly picturesque during sunset, which is often described as 'worthy of a postcard'. The stark white salt crusts and red rocks visible when the lagoons are dry also create striking photographic subjects.

faqs.question: What notable features or viewpoints will I encounter on the route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience the unique landscape of the Laguna del Longar, one of Spain's most extensive salt lagoons. There is a bird observatory with explanatory signs along the route, offering excellent opportunities for wildlife viewing. The area is also known for its stunning photographic opportunities, especially during sunset, and the striking salt crusts when the lagoons are dry.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several local paths. You will run along parts of the Carretera de la Estación de Lillo, Senda Galiana, Camino de las Eras, Camino Viejo de Lillo a Villacañas, Camino del Botón, and Camino del Pozo de los Tablones.

faqs.question: Is the Laguna del Longar – running circular from Lillo dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail is within a natural area, specific regulations for dogs are not detailed. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash in natural reserves, especially given the sensitive bird habitats around the Laguna del Longar, to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all visitors.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: The Laguna del Longar is part of the Reserva Natural de las Lagunas de El Longar, Altillo Grande y Altillo Chica. Generally, natural reserves in Spain do not require permits or charge entrance fees for trail access, but it's always good practice to respect local regulations and signage regarding conservation.

faqs.question: What makes the Laguna del Longar unique among the Lillo salt lakes?
faqs.answer: The Laguna del Longar is unique because, unlike neighboring lagoons that dry out seasonally, it receives treated wastewater from Lillo. This ensures it remains a permanent wetland, providing a consistent habitat for wildlife throughout the year and making it the largest of the salt lakes in the area.
